sketchMechanisms: The Maybe monad's >>= operator silently propagates Nothing through an entire chain, so once any step yields Nothing, every subsequent step is skipped without error.
The `object` declaration creates exactly one instance that can never be duplicated or reconstructed — a language-enforced singleton.
A `volatile` variable tells the compiler the value may change at any moment without warning, so it must be re-read every single time — it can never be cached or assumed stable.
